text: We have synthesized and fully characterized a new family of ruthenium complexes [Ruⁿ(tda)(py)₂]ᵐ⁺ with n=2–4 in which [2,2':6',2''-terpyridine]-6,6''-dicarboxylate 3 (tda²⁻) acts as a pentadentate ligand. The electrochemical behavior, structure, and redox activity of the complexes in solution are determined by X-ray diffraction, NMR, IR and UV-Vis spectroscopy as well as by DFT calculations evidence highly dynamic coordination sphere depending on the oxidation state of the metal: six coordination is observed for Ruᴵⱽ while for Ruᴨᴵ six to seven coordination is readily changed into a twelve coordination number structure for the “Ru-aqua” complexes that are generated upon rearrangement of the complexes in aqueous solution. Among the “Ru-aqua” complexes [Ruᴵⱽ(OH)(tda-κ-N3O)(py)₂]⁺ (4H⁺) is identified as a remarkably efficient water oxidation catalyst that affords turnover frequencies (TOF) amounting to 8,000 s⁻¹ at pH 7.0 as determined by FOWA and even up to 50,000 s⁻¹ at pH 10.0 that are the highest TOF values that have so far been reached with ruthenium-based catalysts under similar conditions. DFT calculations are performed to clarify the factors controlling the catalytic activity of this new complex. According to the calculations, the dangling carboxylate group of the tda²⁻ ligand is found to be a crucial component of the catalytic activity by allowing intramolecular proton transfer that lowers the activation energy barrier for water nucleophilic attack. The calculated free energy activation for the entire catalytic cycle also suggests that the water oxidation activity is highly favorable and provides therefore a new strategy for designing and optimizing highly active molecular catalysts.
